problems.title: 001 · Anagram Detection
problems.statement:
# 001 · Anagram Detection

## Task
Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `true` if `t` is an anagram of `s`
(same characters, same counts), else `false`.

## Signature
`solve(input) -> bool`  where `input = { "s": string, "t": string }`

## Notes
This is a *worked example*: `practice.*` ships with a correct naive solution so
`glifex test 001 <lang>` is green on a fresh clone. Compare it against the hidden
references with `glifex reveal 001 <lang> optimized`. New problems created with
`glifex new` start from blank stubs instead.

php.practice:
<?php
function solve(array $c): bool {
    $s = str_split($c['s']); $t = str_split($c['t']);
    sort($s); sort($t);
    return $s === $t;
}
php.clean:
<?php
function solve(array $c): bool {
    $s = str_split($c['s']); $t = str_split($c['t']);
    sort($s); sort($t);
    return $s === $t;
}
php.optimized:
<?php
function solve(array $c): bool {
    if (strlen($c['s']) !== strlen($c['t'])) return false;
    return count_chars($c['s'], 1) === count_chars($c['t'], 1);
}

problems.title: 002 · Two Sum
problems.statement:
# 002 · Two Sum

## Task
Given `nums` and a `target`, return the indices `[i, j]` (i < j) of the two
numbers adding to `target`. Exactly one solution exists.

## Signature
`solve(input) -> [int, int]`  where `input = { "nums": int[], "target": int }`

php.practice:
<?php
function solve(array $c): array {
    $seen = [];
    foreach ($c['nums'] as $i => $n) {
        $need = $c['target'] - $n;
        if (isset($seen[$need])) return [$seen[$need], $i];
        $seen[$n] = $i;
    }
    return [];
}
php.clean:
<?php
function solve(array $c): array {
    $seen = [];
    foreach ($c['nums'] as $i => $n) {
        $need = $c['target'] - $n;
        if (isset($seen[$need])) return [$seen[$need], $i];
        $seen[$n] = $i;
    }
    return [];
}
php.optimized:
<?php
function solve(array $c): array {
    $seen = [];
    foreach ($c['nums'] as $i => $n) {
        $need = $c['target'] - $n;
        if (isset($seen[$need])) return [$seen[$need], $i];
        $seen[$n] = $i;
    }
    return [];
}

wat.practice:
;; two-sum in WebAssembly Text. Contract (host marshals JSON):
;;   solve(ptr: i32, len: i32, target: f64) -> i64   ;; (i << 32) | j, or -1
(module
  (memory (export "memory") 1)
  (func (export "solve") (param $ptr i32) (param $len i32) (param $target f64) (result i64)
    (local $i i32) (local $j i32) (local $a i32) (local $b i32)
    (block $done
      (loop $outer
        (br_if $done (i32.ge_s (local.get $i) (local.get $len)))
        (local.set $a (i32.load (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (local.get $i) (i32.const 2)))))
        (local.set $j (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1)))
        (loop $inner
          (if (i32.lt_s (local.get $j) (local.get $len))
            (then
              (local.set $b (i32.load (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (local.get $j) (i32.const 2)))))
              (if (f64.eq (f64.add (f64.convert_i32_s (local.get $a)) (f64.convert_i32_s (local.get $b))) (local.get $target))
                (then
                  (return (i64.or
                    (i64.shl (i64.extend_i32_u (local.get $i)) (i64.const 32))
                    (i64.extend_i32_u (local.get $j))))))
              (local.set $j (i32.add (local.get $j) (i32.const 1)))
              (br $inner))))
        (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1)))
        (br $outer)))
    (i64.const -1)))
wat.clean:
;; two-sum in WebAssembly Text. Contract (host marshals JSON):

(module
  (memory (export "memory") 1)
  (func (export "solve") (param $ptr i32) (param $len i32) (param $target f64) (result i64)
    (local $i i32) (local $j i32) (local $a i32) (local $b i32)
    (block $done
      (loop $outer
        (br_if $done (i32.ge_s (local.get $i) (local.get $len)))
        (local.set $a (i32.load (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (local.get $i) (i32.const 2)))))
        (local.set $j (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1)))
        (loop $inner
          (if (i32.lt_s (local.get $j) (local.get $len))
            (then
              (local.set $b (i32.load (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (local.get $j) (i32.const 2)))))
              (if (f64.eq (f64.add (f64.convert_i32_s (local.get $a)) (f64.convert_i32_s (local.get $b))) (local.get $target))
                (then
                  (return (i64.or
                    (i64.shl (i64.extend_i32_u (local.get $i)) (i64.const 32))
                    (i64.extend_i32_u (local.get $j))))))
              (local.set $j (i32.add (local.get $j) (i32.const 1)))
              (br $inner))))
        (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1)))
        (br $outer)))
    (i64.const -1)))
wat.optimized:
;; two-sum in WebAssembly Text. Contract (host marshals JSON):
;;   solve(ptr: i32, len: i32, target: f64) -> i64   ;; (i << 32) | j, or -1
(module
  (memory (export "memory") 1)
  (func (export "solve") (param $ptr i32) (param $len i32) (param $target f64) (result i64)
    (local $i i32) (local $j i32) (local $a i32) (local $b i32)
    (block $done
      (loop $outer
        (br_if $done (i32.ge_s (local.get $i) (local.get $len)))
        (local.set $a (i32.load (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (local.get $i) (i32.const 2)))))
        (local.set $j (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1)))
        (loop $inner
          (if (i32.lt_s (local.get $j) (local.get $len))
            (then
              (local.set $b (i32.load (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (local.get $j) (i32.const 2)))))
              (if (f64.eq (f64.add (f64.convert_i32_s (local.get $a)) (f64.convert_i32_s (local.get $b))) (local.get $target))
                (then
                  (return (i64.or
                    (i64.shl (i64.extend_i32_u (local.get $i)) (i64.const 32))
                    (i64.extend_i32_u (local.get $j))))))
              (local.set $j (i32.add (local.get $j) (i32.const 1)))
              (br $inner))))
        (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1)))
        (br $outer)))
    (i64.const -1)))

problems.title: 003 · Nth Fibonacci
problems.statement:
# 003 · Nth Fibonacci

Return the **nth Fibonacci number**.

`solve(n)` takes a non-negative integer `n` and returns `fib(n)`, where
`fib(0) = 0`, `fib(1) = 1`, and `fib(k) = fib(k-1) + fib(k-2)` for `k ≥ 2`.

The sequence begins `0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21, …`.

This is a **numeric** problem — integers in, an integer out — which is why it's
the home for the WebAssembly Text (WAT) runtime, whose contract is numeric-only.

python.practice:
def solve(case):
    n = case["n"]
    # Return the nth Fibonacci number: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(2)=1, ...
    return 0

ruby.practice:
def solve(input)
  n = input["n"]
  # Return the nth Fibonacci number: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(2)=1, ...
  0
end

wat.practice:
(module
  ;; Export `solve(n)` returning the nth Fibonacci number (i32).
  ;; fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(2)=1, fib(3)=2, ...   WAT is numeric-only.
  (func (export "solve") (param $n i32) (result i32)
    ;; your code here
    (i32.const 0)))
wat.clean:
(module
  ;; nth Fibonacci, iterative.  solve(n) -> fib(n).  fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(2)=1, ...
  (func (export "solve") (param $n i32) (result i32)
    (local $a i32) (local $b i32) (local $t i32)
    (local.set $a (i32.const 0))
    (local.set $b (i32.const 1))
    (block $done
      (loop $loop
        (br_if $done (i32.eqz (local.get $n)))
        (local.set $t (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))
        (local.set $a (local.get $b))
        (local.set $b (local.get $t))
        (local.set $n (i32.sub (local.get $n) (i32.const 1)))
        (br $loop)))
    (local.get $a)))
wat.optimized:
(module
  ;; nth Fibonacci, iterative.  solve(n) -> fib(n).  fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(2)=1, ...
  (func (export "solve") (param $n i32) (result i32)
    (local $a i32) (local $b i32) (local $t i32)
    (local.set $a (i32.const 0))
    (local.set $b (i32.const 1))
    (block $done
      (loop $loop
        (br_if $done (i32.eqz (local.get $n)))
        (local.set $t (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))
        (local.set $a (local.get $b))
        (local.set $b (local.get $t))
        (local.set $n (i32.sub (local.get $n) (i32.const 1)))
        (br $loop)))
    (local.get $a)))

problems.title: DB 001 · Users With No Orders
problems.statement:
# DB 001 · Users With No Orders

## Task
Return the `name` of every user who has never placed an order.

## Order matters
No — the harness compares row sets order-independently unless a problem says otherwise.

## Engines
Runs offline on SQLite (`glifex db test 001`) and on Postgres when hosted.
The schema is dialect-neutral so both engines accept it.
problems.seed:
INSERT INTO users (id, name) VALUES (1,'Ada'), (2,'Bjarne'), (3,'Grace'), (4,'Linus');
INSERT INTO orders (id, user_id, total) VALUES (1,1,42.0), (2,1,10.0), (3,3,99.0);
problems.practice:
SELECT u.name
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.user_id = u.id
WHERE o.id IS NULL;

solutions.clean:
SELECT name FROM users
WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT user_id FROM orders);
solutions.optimized:
-- Anti-join; with an index on orders(user_id) the planner avoids a full scan.
SELECT u.name
FROM users u
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id);

problems.title: FE 001 · Three-Card Flex Row
problems.statement:
# FE 001 · Three-Card Flex Row

## Task
Build a horizontal row of exactly three cards inside a flex container.

Requirements (each is an automated assertion):
1. A `<section class="cards">` container exists and is `display: flex`.
2. It contains exactly **three** elements with class `card`.
3. Each card contains an `<h2>` title.
4. The container has a `gap` of at least `8px`.

## How it's checked
The playground renders your markup live and evaluates `assertions.json`
against the real DOM and computed styles — no pixel-diffing, so results are
deterministic across browsers.
problems.starter:
<!-- Build the three-card flex row described in the problem. -->
<style>
  /* your CSS here */
</style>

<!-- your markup here -->

solutions.clean:
Alpha

First card.

Beta

Second card.

Gamma

Third card.
